2605.10281.title: Drum Synthesis from Expressive Drum Grids via Neural Audio Codecs
2605.10281.paper_summary: Generating realistic drum audio directly from symbolic representations is a challenging task at the intersection of music perception and machine learning. We propose a system that transforms an expressive drum grid, a time-aligned MIDI representation with microtiming and velocity information, into drum audio by predicting discrete codes of a neural audio codec. Our approach uses a Transformer-based model to map the drum grid input to a sequence of codec tokens, which are then converted to waveform audio via a pre-trained codec decoder. We experiment with multiple state-of-the-art neural codecs, namely EnCodec, DAC, and X-Codec, to assess how the choice of audio representation impacts the quality of the generated drums. The system is trained and evaluated on the Expanded Groove MIDI Dataset, E-GMD, a large collection of human drum performances with paired MIDI and audio. We evaluate the fidelity and musical alignment of the generated audio using objective metrics. Overall, our results establish codec-token prediction as an effective route for drum grid-to-audio generation and provide practical insights into selecting audio tokenizers for percussive synthesis.
2605.10281.paper_summary_zh: 生成真實的鼓音頻直接從符號表示是一項位於音樂感知與機器學習交叉點的挑戰性任務。<lb/>我們提出了一個系統，將一個表現豐富的鼓網格（時間對齊的MIDI表示，包含微時間和力度信息）轉換為鼓音頻，通過預測神經音頻編解碼器的離散代碼。<lb/>我們的方法使用基於Transformer的模型，將鼓網格輸入映射到編解碼器標記的序列，然後通過預訓練的編解碼器解碼器將其轉換為波形音頻。<lb/>我們實驗了多個最先進的神經編解碼器，即EnCodec、DAC和X-Codec，以評估音頻表示的選擇如何影響生成鼓的質量。<lb/>該系統在擴展節奏MIDI數據集E-GMD上進行訓練和評估，這是一個大型的人類鼓表演集合，包含配對的MIDI和音頻。<lb/>我們使用客觀指標評估生成音頻的保真度和音樂對齊度。<lb/>總體而言，我們的結果確立了編解碼器標記預測作為鼓網格到音頻生成的有效途徑，並提供了有關選擇打擊合成音頻標記器的實用見解。
2605.10281.author: Konstantinos Soiledis et.al.
2605.10281.authors: Konstantinos Soiledis, Maximos Kaliakatsos-Papakostas, Dimos Makris, Konstantinos Tsamis
